title: payena engleri
intro: Payena engleri, commonly known as the Engler's payena, is a plant species in the family Sapotaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Payena engleri include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects.
side_effects: Payena engleri can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high levels of tannins and saponins. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Payena engleri can lead to allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, the plant's saponins may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially causing adverse effects. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Payena engleri for medicinal purposes to avoid these side effects.
